message:
test(btree,harness,parser): add index seek, concurrency, semantic, and INSERT tests

Add four test files created by other agents during concurrent development:

- crates/fsqlite-btree/src/index_seek_bug_test.rs: Unit test that exercises
  B-tree index insertion under increasing key counts (up to 350 keys) to
  reproduce and guard against off-by-one errors during leaf splits when
  inserted keys fall at leaf boundaries. Note: the mod declaration in lib.rs
  still needs to be added to wire this into the test suite.

- crates/fsqlite-harness/tests/extreme_index_punishment.rs: Integration stress
  test that spawns 20 threads each performing 200 concurrent INSERT operations
  into the same index bucket (category=42) using BEGIN CONCURRENT. Verifies
  both row count and index integrity after the storm. This tests the MVCC
  pipeline, WAL, and B-tree balancing under extreme contention.

- crates/fsqlite-parser/src/semantic_test_hack.rs: Unit test for the semantic
  resolver's rowid resolution — ensures that `SELECT rowid FROM users` resolves
  correctly against a schema with an INTEGER PRIMARY KEY column. Despite the
  "hack" filename, this is a proper #[cfg(test)] module. Note: needs a mod
  declaration in lib.rs to compile.

- tests/insert_test.rs: Integration test for INSERT INTO ... SELECT with
  column reordering (selecting a, b from src and inserting into y, x in dst).
  Verifies that column mapping works correctly across different table schemas.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
